name: Polvoranca Park Loop
name_original: Parque de Polvoranca, Laguna de María Pascuala, Alcorcón — circular desde El Naranjo en Fuenlabrada
description: Hike a moderate 9.6-mile loop through Parque de Polvoranca, featuring the beautiful Laguna de María Pascuala and diverse natural scenery.
region_intro: Hiking around Fuenlabrada offers accessible green spaces and parks within the urban area, providing diverse natural scenery for outdoor activities. The region features local parks with trails, lakes, and olive groves. While Fuenlabrada itself is not mountainous, its location provides convenient access to more extensive natural landscapes in the wider Community of Madrid, including the Sierra de Guadarrama. These areas cater to a range of hiking preferences and fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Fuenlabrada The most popular hiking route is Parque de Polvoranca — circular desde Fuenlabrada, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route features diverse natural scenery and the Laguna de María Pascuala. Another top favourite among local hikers is Barranco del Canto Echado y el Parque del Polvoranca — circular desde Fuenlabrada, a moderate 7.0 miles (11.3 km) path. This trail combines the Barranco del Canto Echado with Polvoranca Park, offering natural surroundings. Local hikers also love the Olivar Park and Valdeserrano Fountain Loop, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through an olive grove, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Fuenlabrada is defined by accessible green spaces, local parks with lakes, and olive groves, with connections to the more rugged landscapes of the Sierra de Guadarrama. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Fuenlabrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 670 hikers have used komoot to explore Fuenlabrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Fuenlabrada?
A: Fuenlabrada offers a wide selection of hiking trails, with over 80 routes available for exploration. These range from easy strolls through local parks to more moderate treks that can be combined for longer distances.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Fuenlabrada?
A: Yes, Fuenlabrada has many easy hiking trails, with over 50 routes classified as easy. Local parks like Parque de Polvoranca and Olivar Park offer accessible paths perfect for beginners or families with children. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1349104813/olivar-park-and-valdeserrano-fountain-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olivar Park and Valdeserrano Fountain Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.1-mile trail through an olive grove, suitable for a pleasant outing.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along the hiking trails in Fuenlabrada?
A: The hiking trails in Fuenlabrada feature diverse natural scenery, including beautiful lakes and green spaces. You can explore areas like the Laguna de María Pascuala within Parque de Polvoranca, or discover the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5149709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de María Pascuala&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has unique natural monuments such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3020485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Invincible Holm Oak&lt;/a&gt; and even a small &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Fuenlabrada?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Fuenlabrada are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1349688638/polvoranca-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 9.6-mile route that allows you to experience the park's diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Fuenlabrada?
A: Many of Fuenlabrada's green spaces and parks are dog-friendly, making them suitable for walks with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and clean up after them to ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Fuenlabrada?
A: The hiking routes in Fuenlabrada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the accessibility of the green spaces, the well-maintained paths, and the peaceful environment offered by local parks like Parque de Polvoranca.

Q: Are there options for longer or more challenging hikes near Fuenlabrada?
A: While Fuenlabrada itself offers mostly easy to moderate trails, its strategic location provides convenient access to more extensive natural landscapes in the wider Community of Madrid. For more challenging treks, you can explore areas like the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, known for its diverse trails and peaks.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Fuenlabrada?
A: Yes, many of the popular parks and green spaces in Fuenlabrada, such as Parque de Polvoranca, offer designated parking areas for visitors. It's generally easy to find parking close to the main entrances of these recreational areas.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Fuenlabrada using public transport?
A: Fuenlabrada has a well-developed public transport network, including metro and bus services, which can provide access to various parts of the city and its surrounding green areas. Many local parks are within reasonable walking distance from public transport stops, making them accessible without a car.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fuenlabrada?
A: Fuenlabrada offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer mornings and evenings can also be enjoyable, while winter offers cooler, crisp air for those who prefer it.

Q: Are there any routes that combine different parks or natural areas?
A: Yes, some routes are designed to connect multiple green spaces. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1354805324/polvoranca-park-and-bosquesur-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park and Bosquesur Trail&lt;/a&gt; combines these two areas, offering a longer moderate hike of over 8 miles through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to look out for during a hike?
A: Beyond the natural features, you might encounter interesting man-made elements like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6220802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeserrano Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the Olivar Park loop. These add a touch of local history and charm to your outdoor experience.
tips: Laguna de la Recomba takes the name given to the first section of the Culebro.

The Polvoranca Park is located in the municipality of Leganés in the southern part of the Community of Madrid, west of the M-406 route. It offers everything you can think of to spend a beautiful day, recreational areas, trails and bicycle paths, bird observatory and you cannot miss visiting the María Pascuala Lake. The Botánico de Polvoranca Park offers a peaceful and pleasant walk through its somewhat neglected but still charming botanical garden. Visitors can enjoy unique species and birdwatching opportunities while rolling along well-defined paths that are accessible for all. The park is recommended for guided tours available at the CEA of Polvoranca and provides a relaxing environment for families to explore nature.
There is a small viewpoint from where you can see the entire lake and you can enjoy a small waterfall.

Nice lake very close to the center of Leganés. It is located within the grounds of the Parque de Polvoranca, a nice place to escape from the city where you can enjoy a variety of paths and paths in good condition to alternate your circuits!

faqs.question: How do I get to the trailhead for this circular route?
faqs.answer: The starting point for this circular route is El Naranjo in Fuenlabrada. The Parque de Polvoranca is well-connected and easily accessible from surrounding municipalities like Leganés, Alcorcón, and Fuenlabrada. You can reach the starting point by public transport, and there are also parking options available near the park for those arriving by car.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied paths and surfaces, including some paved sections and others requiring firm footing. A key characteristic is its minimal elevation change; all slopes, both uphill and downhill, are gentle and do not demand significant effort, making for a generally comfortable hike.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers a refreshing escape into nature. You'll encounter diverse vegetation, including over 400 plant species, themed gardens, an arboretum, and a botanical garden. A central scenic element is the beautiful and emblematic Laguna de María Pascuala, along with two smaller lagoons and five wetlands. You'll also pass by the Parque del Norte and the Laguna de la Recomba.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for all fitness levels?
faqs.answer: The specific route is rated as moderate hiking, typically requiring good physical condition. However, many paths within Parque de Polvoranca are considered simple and suitable for walking, cycling, or even horseback riding, with some being rated as easy or for all fitness levels. If you're looking for a less strenuous option, you can explore the park's easier internal routes.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Parque de Polvoranca is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially around wildlife areas like the lagoons, and to clean up after them to maintain the park's natural beauty for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. These seasons are also ideal for birdwatching around the lagoons. Summers can be hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during those months.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for Parque de Polvoranca?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access Parque de Polvoranca or to hike this trail. The park is open to the public, offering free access to its natural beauty and recreational facilities.

faqs.question: What notable features or points of interest can I find along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty of the lagoons, such as the prominent Laguna de María Pascuala, you can also observe the ruins of the San Pedro church, dating back to 1655. The park also features an Environmental Education Center and various recreational facilities like sports courts and a bike circuit. Don't miss the Polvoranca Park Waterfall.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ruta Verde 4.3 Parque Polvoranca, Ruta Verde 4.4 Fuenlabrada - Parque Polvoranca - Alcorcón, and the RC10: Fuenlabrada – Bosque Sur – Polvoranca – Parque Forestal Las Presillas (Alcorcón) – Colonia Jardín (Madrid), among others.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children or strollers?
faqs.answer: While the overall route is rated moderate, Parque de Polvoranca itself has many paths designed to be accessible for individuals with reduced mobility or those with strollers. If you're hiking with young children or a stroller, you might prefer to stick to the wider, more easily accessible paths within the park rather than the full circular route.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I spot around the Laguna de María Pascuala?
faqs.answer: The park, especially around the lagoons like Laguna de María Pascuala, is an excellent spot for birdwatching. You can expect to see varied avian fauna, including ducks and other water birds. The diverse aquatic environments support a rich ecosystem, so keep an eye out for different species.
